atomic_request: Provide a complete point-to-point response document.
route_c_action: Build an atomised evidence-linked response after manuscript freeze.
minimum_evidence: response letter; closure matrix; manuscript anchors
closure_rule: Every substantive request has a direct response evidence and exact location.

atomic_request: Provide a revised Word manuscript with changes visibly highlighted.
route_c_action: Generate anonymous tracked or single-colour highlighted Word file and parity-check it against the clean version.
closure_rule: Visible changes remain readable and anonymous.

atomic_request: Limit the title to eight words.
route_c_action: Use the frozen eight-word title.
closure_rule: Title contains exactly eight words.

atomic_request: Keep the revised manuscript within 10,000 words.
route_c_action: Use a 9,300-word internal ceiling and automated word-count audit.
closure_rule: Hard count does not exceed 10,000.

atomic_request: Avoid unnecessary self-citation and prior JCIS citation.
route_c_action: Use external direct literature for novelty and cite project releases only for availability or indispensable provenance.
closure_rule: Every self-citation has an indispensable evidential role.

atomic_request: Do not change authorship.
route_c_action: Freeze the single-author identity and order.
closure_rule: Author count identity and order are unchanged.

atomic_request: Apply JCIS reference and manuscript style.
route_c_action: Reconstruct structure terminology references figures and tables under a final style audit.
closure_rule: No unresolved style blocker.

atomic_request: Do not number headings or subheadings.
route_c_action: Use unnumbered headings in every manuscript version.
closure_rule: Numbered manuscript headings equal zero.

atomic_request: Add detailed examples of health data exchanged.
route_c_action: Open with and execute a concrete synthetic DiagnosticReport disclosure case.
closure_rule: The case contains concrete clinically plausible values and exact versions without real-patient wording.

atomic_request: Explain what Merkle trees are.
route_c_action: Add a concise domain-specific explanation of leaves nodes roots and proof paths.
closure_rule: Explanation matches the executed local algorithm and avoids generic blockchain rhetoric.

atomic_request: Explain the purpose of Merkle trees in this artefact.
route_c_action: Tie inclusion and retained-checkpoint consistency to precise dispute questions and non-claims.
closure_rule: Purpose is expressed as bounded inclusion/consistency evidence not truth or completeness.

atomic_request: Explain the process steps and tools in greater detail.
route_c_action: Use an Input–Processing–Output pipeline exact tool versions and a reproduction path.
closure_rule: A reader can identify inputs operations outputs and validators without repository guesswork.

atomic_request: Extend the state-of-the-art section.
route_c_action: Replace the catalogue with a direct comparator synthesis covering FHIR/BALP consent systems healthcare ledgers SCITT and COSE Receipts.
closure_rule: Novelty is bounded by direct current sources and standards.

atomic_request: Make table and figure text readable.
route_c_action: Reduce the main visual set redesign at final size and move large matrices to the supplement.
closure_rule: All text is readable at final page scale and no visual is a screenshot.

atomic_request: Include a complete evidence entity or resource with concrete data.
route_c_action: Publish a fully populated signed envelope and its FHIR projection from the hero case.
closure_rule: The example is complete synthetic and byte-traceable.

atomic_request: Explain where and how encryption is used.
route_c_action: Separate hashing signatures TLS and optional at-rest controls; claim encryption only where executed.
closure_rule: Hashing is never described as encryption and unexecuted controls are not results.

atomic_request: Summarise the total overhead added to EHR exchange.
route_c_action: Replace isolated timing language with preregistered local B0–B2 processing and byte increments.
minimum_evidence: raw runs; p50 p95 p99; paired increments
closure_rule: Wording remains `local reference-pipeline increment` and not production EHR overhead.

atomic_request: Replace the difficult title with a readily comprehensible title.
route_c_action: Use the frozen eight-word reader-centred title.
closure_rule: Title identity is unchanged across portal and manuscript files.

atomic_request: Rewrite the abstract as a concise self-contained summary.
route_c_action: Write the abstract after results freeze using problem objective method findings and conclusion.
closure_rule: Every abstract finding appears identically in Results.

atomic_request: Add recent research references where relevant.
route_c_action: Add 2022–2026 direct consent audit privacy and transparency sources while retaining justified foundational DSR sources.
closure_rule: Recent sources are direct and DOI-verified rather than decorative.

atomic_request: Clarify how the validity or verification of the study is established.
route_c_action: Separate standards validation implementation verification security properties empirical conclusion validity and non-claims.
minimum_evidence: validator reports; mutation reports; experiment protocol; release evidence
closure_rule: Each validity class has an executed method or an explicit not-established status.

atomic_request: Define specialist terms for interdisciplinary readers.
route_c_action: Use a canonical terminology sheet inline first-use definitions and a reduced acronym set.
closure_rule: Every retained technical term is defined before abbreviated use.

atomic_request: Write full names before abbreviations including FHIR BALP and SUSHI.
route_c_action: Expand official names at first occurrence and describe SUSHI by function without inventing an expansion.
manuscript_destination: Abstract exclusions; first use in main text
closure_rule: Undefined acronyms equal zero.

atomic_request: Deepen the background review rather than listing selected papers.
route_c_action: Organise prior work by representation custody consent binding authentication backend assurance and completeness.
closure_rule: Each stream ends in a specific gap or boundary relevant to an RQ.

atomic_request: Correct inappropriate citation grouping such as Bennett et al. 2023 2024 2025.
route_c_action: Separate the peer-reviewed conversion article full dataset and demo dataset by evidential function.
closure_rule: No bundled pseudo-citation remains.

atomic_request: Describe sample datasets and metadata.
route_c_action: Document source type version seed licence selected resources transformations and payload treatment for each executed case.
closure_rule: Every data input can be located and its role distinguished from a proxy.

atomic_request: Explain what data or information was designed and how it was used.
route_c_action: Present the field registry and hero-case boundary with input source portable references commitments and exclusions.
closure_rule: The paper distinguishes clinical source data from portable audit facts.

atomic_request: Shift emphasis from background to actual methodology experiment and results.
route_c_action: Apply the frozen word budget and move detailed registries to supplement or repository.
closure_rule: Methods plus Results receive more space than generic background.

atomic_request: Add a diagram of the model or system configuration.
route_c_action: Create a cross-organisational evidence-flow figure grounded in the hero case.
closure_rule: Figure shows actors data paths custody and outputs at readable scale.

atomic_request: Replace the overly abstract architecture figure.
route_c_action: Retire the legacy figure and use the concrete Hospital A–Hospital B flow.
closure_rule: No abstract box has an unexplained role.

atomic_request: Use noun-phrase figure subjects and readable fonts.
route_c_action: Use short noun-phrase labels vector output and final-size visual inspection.
closure_rule: All labels remain readable in the generated reviewer PDF.

atomic_request: Use TrustEvidence and hyphenated terms consistently.
route_c_action: Freeze canonical terminology and prohibit Trust-Evidence.
closure_rule: Prohibited variants equal zero.

atomic_request: Explain the research design and execution in accessible language.
route_c_action: Use the Route C RQs build-evaluate sequence and an IPO description tied to one case.
closure_rule: A non-specialist can trace one input to one output.

atomic_request: Do not describe missing technologies as the research environment.
route_c_action: Replace the legacy absence list with the actual executed environment and excluded scope.
closure_rule: Methods state what ran; Limitations state what did not.

atomic_request: Present Input Processing and Output explicitly.
route_c_action: Publish the frozen validate-classify-minimise-commit-canonicalise-sign-project-append-verify chain.
closure_rule: Inputs outputs assumptions and steps match code and case artefacts.

atomic_request: Explain whether the process figure is an algorithm or flowchart.
route_c_action: Label it as a processing pipeline and supplement it with concise pseudocode.
closure_rule: Caption and text state purpose and interpretation.

atomic_request: Define variables inputs outputs assumptions and operation sequence.
route_c_action: Use an algorithm box with typed inputs exact outputs and explicit verification assumptions.
closure_rule: No variable or state requirement is implicit.

atomic_request: Use case examples and sample metadata in the evaluation.
route_c_action: Evaluate the disclosure case a wearable batch and controlled negative variants.
closure_rule: Every reported result is linked to a named case or workload.

atomic_request: Refer to and explain every figure in the text.
route_c_action: Use a figure registry and first-reference audit.
closure_rule: Every figure is introduced before or at its first appearance.

atomic_request: Order Results to match methods and research questions.
route_c_action: Report RQ1 through RQ4 in the same order as Evaluation.
closure_rule: No result precedes its method.

atomic_request: Keep Results objective and move interpretation to Discussion.
route_c_action: Use one finding per paragraph and reserve explanations and implications for Discussion.
closure_rule: No speculative or promotional result paragraph remains.

atomic_request: Ensure numerical consistency across text tables and figures.
route_c_action: Generate all numbers from one machine-readable registry.
closure_rule: Numerical discrepancies equal zero.

atomic_request: Avoid repeating every table value in prose.
route_c_action: Report headline findings and direct the reader to the table for detail.
closure_rule: Prose interprets structure but does not duplicate tables.

atomic_request: Rewrite the paper concisely and from the reader's perspective.
route_c_action: Reconstruct from a new blueprint rather than patching legacy prose.
closure_rule: Hero case and RQ flow remain visible throughout.

atomic_request: Define a clear research contribution.
route_c_action: Use the frozen boundary-model and executable-profile contribution architecture.
closure_rule: Contribution is distinguishable from standards and primitives.

atomic_request: Provide methodological rigour and evidence-driven conclusions.
route_c_action: Use explicit RQs cases validators mutations B0–B2 protocol and release evidence.
closure_rule: Every conclusion maps to executed evidence.

atomic_request: Critically synthesise prior literature and identify the gap.
route_c_action: Use the direct dimensional synthesis and current standards boundary.
closure_rule: No descriptive literature catalogue remains.

atomic_request: Improve structure and logical flow.
route_c_action: Use the hero-case to RQ to evaluation narrative arc.
closure_rule: Transitions and section functions are explicit.

atomic_request: Rewrite the abstract to report problem method findings and contribution.
route_c_action: Write the abstract last from the frozen results registry.
closure_rule: No merely descriptive abstract remains.

atomic_request: Add literature on generative AI programming occupations labour markets and AI-assisted programming.
route_c_action: Record an apparent manuscript mismatch; address the general literature concern only with domain-relevant sources.
closure_rule: No unrelated AI or labour-market content is inserted.

atomic_request: Explain specialist acronyms for an interdisciplinary JCIS audience.
route_c_action: Reduce and define the acronym set.
closure_rule: Undefined specialist acronyms equal zero.

atomic_request: Review direct prior work on separating verifiable audit evidence from clinical records in greater depth.
route_c_action: Use consent implementations healthcare ledgers EHR access auditing privacy reviews and generic transparency standards as direct comparators.
closure_rule: The paper addresses both supportive and adverse comparator evidence.

atomic_request: State exactly whether TrustEvidence is an architecture boundary abstraction framework or integration.
route_c_action: Define it as a boundary model with an executable reference profile.
closure_rule: A single identity is used across manuscript repository and response.

atomic_request: Clarify novelty relative to FHIR AuditEvent Provenance and Consent.
route_c_action: Acknowledge their native capabilities and locate the bounded addition in field selection custody binding and evaluation.
minimum_evidence: FHIR delta matrix; official definitions; C3 mapping
closure_rule: No caricature of FHIR is used.

atomic_request: Clarify novelty relative to prior cryptographic-integrity integrations.
route_c_action: Attribute signatures Merkle algorithms SCITT and COSE Receipts to prior standards and narrow novelty to the healthcare profile.
minimum_evidence: SCITT/FHIR delta; comparator matrix; threat model
closure_rule: No primitive or priority claim remains.

atomic_request: Discuss scalability to large healthcare networks.
route_c_action: Report only bounded local workload evidence and explain unmeasured network organisational and persistent-state dimensions.
closure_rule: No large-network scalability claim is made.

atomic_request: Discuss realistic hospital deployability.
route_c_action: Identify required integration points keys validators custody and operations while stating that no hospital deployment was executed.
closure_rule: Reference deployability is not equated with hospital readiness.

atomic_request: Explain interoperability beyond existing FHIR implementations.
route_c_action: Define the added target as audit-evidence interoperability rather than improvement of FHIR semantic interoperability.
minimum_evidence: construct definition; mapping and round trip
closure_rule: Claim concerns portable evidence semantics and binding only.

atomic_request: Discuss whether the approach reduces auditing cost and complexity.
route_c_action: Measure local processing and bytes; inventory added components; do not claim organisational cost reduction.
closure_rule: Cost-reduction wording is absent unless directly measured.

atomic_request: Discuss accountability transparency and trust implications.
route_c_action: Distinguish attributable evidence bounded inclusion/consistency and unmeasured organisational trust.
closure_rule: Trust is not reported as an outcome.
